data.title: Sarepta to refinance $291M of existing notes due 2027
data.description:
• Sarepta Therapeutics (SRPT) has agreed to privately exchange about $291.4M of its existing 1.25% convertible notes due 2027 for an equal amount of new 4.875% convertible notes due 2030, plus approximately $31.6M in cash.
  • The exchange is expected to close on or about December 18, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions. Following the closing, around $158.6 million of the existing convertible notes will remain outstanding with terms unchanged.
  • The new convertible notes will be issued as part of the same series as the ~$602 million of 4.875% convertible senior notes due 2030 originally issued in August 2025.
  • Following the closing of the exchange, the principal amount of the company’s 4.875% convertible senior notes due 2030 will total $893.4 million.
  • SRPT -2.7% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Oracle slumps, but analysts look past weak results towards AI growth
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) was in focus on Thursday as Wall Street firms defended the IT giant after it reported mixed results, and said the growth from artificial intelligence will be surprising in the years to come.

Shares fell more than 12% in premarket trading, bringing down other AI-related stocks such as Microsoft (MSFT), Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and others.

Wedbush Securities analyst Dan Ives said investors should look past the weaker-than-expected revenue and at the $523B in remaining performance obligations, or RPO, which topped Wall Street's forecast of $500B. 

“Oracle added impressively $69 billion in incremental RPO (vs. $317 billion last quarter including the $300 billion OpenAI deal) and this compares to RPO of $8 billion, $33 billion, and $2 billion in the prior quarters,” Ives wrote in a note to clients. “This is the important number we are laser focused on vs. the actual top-line result this quarter or guidance next quarter. Taking a step back, this should translate into eye-popping revenue growth of 17%+ in FY26 and then roughly 35% in FY27 and ~45% in FY28 as conversion of AI backlog takes place over the coming years.”

For fiscal 2026, Oracle reaffirmed its revenue guidance of $67B, in-line with the $67B estimate. The company also said on its earnings call that the additional remaining performance obligations can be monetized in the future and eventually convert to revenue in short order. Oracle expects roughly $4B in additional revenue in fiscal 2027, management added.

Additionally, the concerns about the debt levels that Oracle will need to raise to fund its massive AI-infused expansion are likely overblown, Ives added. 

“Furthermore, Oracle will likely need to raise less than the $100 billion in debt to service the Capex needed for the New Cloud ARR/RPO and could be significantly under this number when all is said it done in our view,” Ives added. “This is naturally a focus point of investors, and we believe it's a high class problem that Oracle is dealing with on the AI demand front.”

BNP Paribas analyst Stefan Slowinski agreed and said the results, particularly the revenue from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, were a “relief.” 

“That said, with concerns that there may have been delays in the key Abilene datacenter that would cause a miss, the 66% [growth in constant currency] is somewhat of a relief to us,” Slowinski wrote in a note to clients. “[Free cash flow] fell to -$10bn in FQ2 (driven by $12bn of capex, ~46% ahead of [consensus]) indicating that OCI growth will likely come through strong in H2, and thus no datacenter build delays are visible, which also appears as a relief.”

Slowinski has a Buy rating and $290 price target on Oracle.

data.title: North Shore Uranium announces $3 million private placement
data.description:
• North Shore Uranium (NSU:CA) announced on Thursday a non-brokered private placement offering for gross proceeds of up to $3 million through the issuance of units of the company at $0.25 per unit.
  • Each unit consists of one common share and one-half of one share purchase warrant. Each warrant entitles the holder to purchase one common share at $0.40 per warrant share for a period of two years from closing of the offering.
  • The company said that the net proceeds of the offering will be used for exploration of the Rio Puerco uranium project in New Mexico, continued exploration of its Saskatchewan uranium properties, the costs of the offering and for general working capital.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Beneficient announces reverse stock split to regain compliance with Nasdaq’s minimum bid price
data.description:
• Beneficient (BENF) on Thursday announced a 1-for-8 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock, par value $0.001 per share, and its Class B common stock, par value $0.001 per share.
  • Beneficient’s Class A Common Stock will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “BENF” and will begin trading on a split-adjusted basis when the market opens on December 15, 2025. 
  • The reverse stock split is intended to enable Beneficient to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement for continued listing on Nasdaq.
  • As a result of the reverse stock split, the number of Beneficient’s authorized shares of Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock will be proportionally reduced from 5,000,000,000 and 250,000, respectively, to 625M and 31,250, respectively.
  • Beneficient expects to have its number of shares of issued and outstanding Class A Common Stock and Class B Common Stock to decrease from about 110.75M and 239,256 pre-split shares outstanding, respectively, to approximately 13.84M and 29,907 post-split shares outstanding, respectively, as a result of the reverse stock split.
  • BENF -2.0% premarket to $0.9996.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AeroVironment secures $4.8M contract from United States Coast Guard
data.description:
• AeroVironment (AVAV) announced on Thursday that it has been awarded a $4.8 million United States Coast Guard contract through its wholly owned subsidiary, VideoRay, to deliver Mission Specialist Defender remotely operated vehicles (ROVs) as part of the Service’s Force Design 2028 modernization initiative.
  • The company said that the Mission Specialist Defender remotely operated vehicle is a modular underwater system built for precision, payload capacity, and mission adaptability.
  • “These unmanned systems provide increased domain awareness, mitigating risk and enhancing mission success as the Coast Guard continues to operate in hazardous environments,” said Anthony Antognoli, the Coast Guard’s first RAS program executive officer, in a separate release issued by the U.S. Coast Guard in September 2025. 
  • AVAV +0.04% premarket to $245.35.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Prudential Financial to effect up to $1B stock buyback program
data.description:
Prudential Financial (PRU) announced on Wednesday an up to $1B share repurchase program, effective January 1, 2026, to December 31, 2026.

The company said the buybacks may be executed in the open market, through derivatives, accelerated repurchases, and other negotiated transactions.

data.title: As Oracle slides after results, see the top SA Quant-rated systems software stocks
data.description:
Oracle (ORCL) stock slid in Thursday premarket trading, as investors were disappointed by the cloud software giant's current quarter guidance and increased spending forecast for 2026.

The company now expects fiscal 2026 capital expenditure to be about $15B  higher than its previous outlook. Much of Oracle's (ORCL) capex is spent on data centers. Shares of the company were -12.5% ahead of the opening bell.

In light of Oracle's (ORCL) underwhelming results, Seeking Alpha compiled a list of top systems software stocks and ranked them according to their Quant ratings. 

Telos (TLS) and Appian (APPN) top the list with Strong Buy ratings of 4.81 and 4.54, respectively. UiPath (PATH) follows with a Buy rating of 4.13. Major industry players like Microsoft (MSFT) and ServiceNow (NOW) also appear on the list with Hold ratings, demonstrating the comprehensive range of companies represented in the systems software sector. Oracle (ORCL) is ranked sixth on the list.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating system grades stocks based on their relative performance on various critical quantitative measures, including valuation, growth, stock momentum, and profitability. Ratings are given on a scale from 1 to 5, with any rating of 3.5 or above considered bullish and any rating of 2.5 or below considered bearish.

Here is the complete list of systems software stocks ranked by their Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings:

  1. Telos (TLS), Quant Rating: 4.81, Strong Buy.

  3. UiPath (PATH), Quant Rating: 4.13, Buy.

data.title: Goodfood co-founder and COO Neil Cuggy steps down; Selim Bassoul named Executive Chair
Crude pares gains after tanker seizure in Venezuela. What's next?
data.description:
The United States' strategy when it comes to Venezuela is rapidly evolving from economic pressure to armed force. A sanctioned supertanker was just seized by American troops off the country's coast, with American commandos rappelling from helicopters and taking the boat by force. When asked what would happen to the oil onboard, President Trump replied, "We keep it, I guess," while issuing a separate warning to Colombia for "producing a lot of drugs." Chevron flies Venezuela staff to oil sites despite U.S. warning

Operation Southern Spear: The U.S. military buildup in the Caribbean intensified at the end of the summer, and by September, airstrikes were already being conducted on small vessels that were identified as "narco-terrorists." In a cabinet meeting last week, Trump revealed that airstrikes would begin on land soon, after referring to the cartels as the "ISIS of the Western Hemisphere." While drug traffickers appear to be the No. 1 priority, the latest oil tanker seizure suggests there might be more at play, especially after Trump declared the days of Venezuelan leader Nicolás Maduro "are numbered."

WTI crude futures (CL1:COM) initially climbed on the news, but quickly gave back those gains and are now down 1.5% to $57 per barrel. "Tensions are continuing to move up the escalation ladder and introduce some short-term supply risk," CIBC Private Wealth Group senior energy trader Rebecca Babin wrote in a research note. "That said, given the administration's clear desire to keep oil and gasoline prices contained, the market is only assigning a small risk premium. Any potential disruption is still being viewed as short-lived."

Outlook: Shadow fleet shippers are likely to be more hesitant in dealing with Venezuelan crude going forward, which could be the beginning of a naval blockade around the country. It's also likely to hit hard, as crude accounts for the vast majority of export earnings for Venezuela, which has the world's largest proven oil reserves. Interestingly, the type of heavy, sour crude produced in Venezuela is the same type that's needed for many U.S. refineries, which has been in tighter supply, attributable in part to sanctions imposed on Russia. Why The U.S. Cannot Use Its Own Oil

data.title: Altria says CEO Billy Gifford to retire, names Sal Mancuso as successor
data.description:
• Altria Group (MO) on Thursday said that CEO Billy Gifford has decided to retire, effective May 14, 2026.
  • Billy has served as Altria’s CEO since 2020.
  • Altria’s Board of Directors has elected Salvatore (Sal) Mancuso to serve as Altria’s CEO upon Billy’s retirement.
  • To facilitate a smooth transition, Billy plans to serve as a consultant to Altria upon his retirement through at least the end of 2026.
  • Additionally, the Board elected Heather Newman to serve as Altria’s Chief Financial Officer, effective May 14, 2026.
